formulas.name: Information Technology Act, 2000
formulas.notes: This act regulates electronic commerce in India and provides a legal framework for cybercrime investigations.

formulas.name: Cybercrime Laws
formulas.notes: The Information Technology Act, 2000, makes provisions for cybercrime laws in India, including penalties for various offenses.

formulas.name: Section 43A of IT Act
formulas.notes: This section provides for compensation to be paid by a body corporate to any person for loss or injury caused due to any unauthorized access or disclosure made by a computer resource.

formulas.name: Section 66A of IT Act
formulas.notes: This section deals with the punishment for sending offensive messages through a computer or other communication device.

formulas.name: Section 67 of IT Act
formulas.notes: This section provides for publishing or transmitting obscene material in electronic form.

formulas.name: Section 92 of IT Act
formulas.notes: This section states that the provisions of this Act apply to any offence or contravention committed outside India by any person, if the act or contravention has an effect in India.

formulas.name: Section 79 of IT Act
formulas.notes: This section provides for liability of intermediaries such as social media platforms and online service providers for third-party content.

formulas.name: E-commerce Regulations
formulas.notes: The Information Technology Act, 2000, regulates e-commerce activities, including online payment systems, digital signatures, and data protection.
